data.title: Dollar hits year-to-date lows as bulls get nervous; yen retreats from high
data.description:
Key points:
  • Yen jumps on Japan inflation before retreating
  • Sterling, Australian and New Zealand dollars at year-to-date highs
  • Euro firm ahead of German election

The yen retreated on Friday from a 2-1/2 month high hit after of a jump in Japanese inflation, while the dollar was set for its third weekly drop in a row as traders calculated the start of Donald Trump's second term has been mostly bluster on the tariff front.

The yen broke through chart resistance at 150 per dollar overnight and strengthened as far as 149.285 per dollar, while Japanese government bonds were sold off after Japan recorded core inflation running at its fastest pace for 19 months in January.

But the yen and yields fell back as Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda warned the central bank could step up government bond buying if long-term interest rates rise sharply.

The euro was up 0.8% overnight and was steady in Asia at $1.0504, with traders awaiting an election in Germany on the weekend where polls point to a conservative coalition win.

The dollar nursed broad losses as bulls who had built up big long positions in anticipation of a trade war have backed off while Trump equivocates about tariffs.

Trump has slapped an additional 10% tariff on Chinese goods and announced plans to reimpose steel and aluminium levies from his first term, but suspended threatened tariffs on Canada and Mexico while numerous others remain - as of yet - only threats.

"It was a very one-sided trade and very heavy long positioning," said Jason Wong, strategist at BNZ in Wellington.

"Some of those longs are becoming impatient because the only thing he has done is put (a) 10% (tariff) on China - so the market is taking some of that money off the table."

Wong said evidence that Japan's inflation was picking up - the core consumer price index rose to an annual 3.2% against expectations for 3.1% - also bolstered the case for higher Japanese rates at a time when the rest of the world might be cutting, and so was buoying the yen.

The yen has gained around 3.4% on the dollar through February so far and interest rate markets have priced in a 25 basis point rate hike by September (0#JPYIRPR).

The yen tumbled after BOJ Governor Ueda's warning to as low as 150.73 per dollar before paring some losses as the BOJ chief reiterated more rate hikes could come if the economic picture supports it. The dollar was last up 0.42% at 150.27 yen.

Yen weakness on Friday was likely more due to profit taking rather than a reassessment of rate-hike bets, but the upside for yen below 150 per dollar appeared limited for the time being, said Nomura Securities currency strategist Jin Moteki.

"BOJ officials could push back market expectations," if market pricing becomes too aggressive and the yen appreciates rapidly, while additional U.S. tariffs could boost the dollar, he said.

A remark from Trump that a trade deal with China was possible lifted the trade-sensitive Australian and New Zealand dollars.

Comments from his Treasury secretary, Scott Bessent, that the administration had no plans to increase long-dated debt sales put downward pressure on yields and the dollar.

The dollar index on Thursday touched its lowest for 2025 at 106.29 and was last at 106.40.

The Aussie and kiwi are trading at their highest levels this year despite rate cuts on both sides of the Tasman this week, and New Zealand's central bank flagging more to come.

Australia is in a position where there could be some more decreases in interest rates but policymakers have to be cautious, the country's top central banker said on Friday.

The kiwi touched $0.5772 on Friday morning, while the Aussie has broken above 64 cents for the first time this year and touched $0.6409.

Trump said Chinese President Xi Jinping would visit the U.S., without giving a timeline, which sent up the yuan to a one-month high in onshore trade on Thursday on hopes that a deal might be reached to avert an all-out trade war.

In offshore trade the yuan was steady at 7.2385 per dollar on Friday.

Sterling touched its highest since mid-December at $1.2674.

Later on Friday purchasing managers' index indicators are released worldwide.

data.title: Standard Chartered: Proposes Final Dividend of 28 Cents Per Share >2888.HK
Hong Kong Shares Extend Gains Midday — Market Talk
data.description: Hong Kong shares are extending gains at midday amid positive sentiment lifted by tech earnings and the recent hype about DeepSeek. Alibaba's Hong Kong-listed shares are up 13% following its strong earnings and the tech giant's long-term growth potential on AI. Tencent and Xiaomi are up 4.5% and 4.8%, respectively. China Unicom gains 12.3% and Lenovo Group is up 12.05%. Meanwhile, the top decliners include Tingyi Holding, which is down 3.1% and Sands China 2.8% lower. The benchmark Hang Seng Index is up 2.0% to 23238.42 and the Hang Seng Tech Index is 4.7% higher. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: ANZ Research Says New Zealand Economy On Pace for Gradual Recovery Amid Low Interest Rates; OCR Likely to Settle at 3%
data.description:
The New Zealand economy is on pace for a gradual recovery as lower interest rates stir activity and contain inflationary pressures, ANZ Research said in a Thursday note.

"[C]ome 2025 the economy is starting to get back on its feet," analysts said, citing high-frequency activity indicators such as consumer confidence, job ads, and house sales data as proof.

ANZ Research said the Reserve Bank of New Zealand has done enough to ensure progress in underlying disinflation is sustained. It noted that interest rates are on course to reach unrestrictive level, but that it remains to be seen whether or not monetary stimulus is needed.

Monetary stimulus is loosely assessed as having an official cash rate below 3%, but ANZ Research said it remains a possibility that 3% is already a stimulatory level.

The firm expects the central bank to cut the rate further until it reaches 3%.

Etherstack Issues Clarification on Capital Structure
data.description:
Etherstack clarified that in its April 24, 2024, announcement it erroneously referred to fully-paid ordinary shares being canceled, when in fact it was the firm's Chess depository interests in respect of those ordinary shares that were canceled, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The ordinary shares that were linked to the canceled Chess depository interests remain on issue, and are currently being held by the depositary, the filing said.

Its shares fell 3% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: Solarium Green Energy Bags New Orders Worth INR559 Million; Shares Up 5%
data.description:
Solarium Green Energy has secured orders worth 558.5 million Indian rupees from NTPC Vidyut Vyapar Nigam, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The contracts are for the development of 10,267 KW grid, 6929 KW grid and 27 KW grid-connected rooftop solar projects at various locations of central armed forces and departments under the Ministry of Home Affairs, India in the states of Madhya

Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Odisha, and Uttar Pradesh.

The company also bagged four orders from various undisclosed client companies, amounting to 157.1 million rupees for the supply of Bifacial Topcon Half-cut 132 cells- Glass to Glass PV module - TRINA.

Shares of the company jumped 5% in recent trade.

data.title: HK-listed Laopu Gold surges on robust earnings outlook; rivals climb
data.description:
** Shares of Laopu Gold jump as much as 20.8% to HK$566, post their highest and biggest one-day pct gain since debut on June 28, 2024

** The Chinese gold jewellery maker and retailer expects to record net profit of 1.4 billion-1.5 billion yuan ($193.37 million-$207.18 million) for 2024, up 236%-260% from a year ago

** Co, with current market capitalisation of HK$90.58 bln ($11.66 bln), says the strong performance is due to expansion of brand influence, which resulted in higher revenue from existing boutiques across online and offline channels

** Brokers said recent strength in gold price also aidded demand for the precious metal

** Gold set for eighth weekly gain as tariff threats boost safe-haven demand

** Shares of rival Chow Tai Fook Jewellery , with market capitalisation of HK$78.8 billion, climb 3.8% to HK$8.13, highest since October 8, 2024; Luk Fook gains 1.2%

** Hang Seng Commerce & Industry Index (.HSNC) surges 4.4x%, Hang Seng Index rises 2.9%

** Shares of Laopu Gold has gained 94.2%, YTD

($1 = 7.2401 Chinese yuan)

($1 = 7.7707 Hong Kong dollars)

data.title: Bendigo and Adelaide Bank Cuts Home Loan Rates Following Reserve Bank Move
data.description:
Bendigo and Adelaide Bank decreased interest rate by 0.25% per annum for home loan customers on variable rate loans as of March 7, according to a Friday statement from the bank.

This comes after Australia's central bank cut interest rates by a quarter point to 4.1% on Tuesday, the first cut in more than four years.

data.title: Chancellor Urges Financial Firms to Invest in UK's Emerging Markets
data.description:
UK's Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rachel Reeves, called some of the nation's biggest financial services companies such as Aviva (AV.L), HSBC , and Schroders to invest in the country's emerging markets.

During a meeting on Thursday, Reeves met with the companies and encouraged them to collaborate with development institutions including the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development, or EBRD, and British International Investment to boost sustainable sovereign debt financing in these markets.

At the meeting, Reeves also signed a memorandum of understanding with EBRD President Odile Renaud-Basso to formalize the collaboration to support private capital mobilization for development.

data.title: 11Street aims for full-year EBITDA profitability this year
data.description: South Korea’s major e-commerce platform 11Street Co. a subsidiary of SK square Co., is accelerating its profitability strategy, with its chief executive officer setting a goal to achieve full-year EBITDA profitability in 2025. 11Street announced on Thursday that during a town hall meeting held on Wednesday at its Gwangmyeong headquarters, its CEO Ahn Jung-eun outlined plans to strengthen core commerce capabilities and focus on profit-driven operations. “Through the reinforcement of our fundamental commerce competitiveness and a profitability-focused approach, we achieved an annual EBITDA surplus of over 10 billion won ($6.96 million) in our core open marketplace segment last year,” Ahn said. “Building on this momentum, we aim to turn the entire company, including both the open marketplace and retail (direct purchase) divisions, into an EBITDA-positive operation this year.”

data.title: Naver unveils lighter, more powerful AI model
data.description: South Korean internet giant Naver Corp. has unveiled an optimized version of its artificial intelligence (AI) model, HyperCLOVA X, reducing its size to 40 percent of the previous version while improving performance. The updated model also features enhanced multimodal capabilities, allowing it to process and understand images and videos. Naver announced on Thursday that it had completed updates to its flagship HyperCLOVA X model, rolling it out internally. Despite having only 40 percent of the parameters compared to its predecessor, the new model outperformed the previous version across 19 benchmark tests, including Korean, English, coding, and math. In Massive Multitask Language Understanding (MMLU), a widely recognized AI benchmark, the model achieved a 79.6 percent accuracy rate, placing it on par with Anthropic’s Claude 3 Sonnet and Alibaba’s Qwen-32B. While Naver has not disclosed the exact model size, it claims that HyperCLOVA X demonstrates language understanding comparable to leading global AI models. With its enhanced multimodal capabilities, the AI model now processes visual and auditory data, enabling visual question-answering and advanced chart and graph interpretation. By streamlining its structure and reducing parameter size, Naver has also improved operational efficiency by more than 50 percent. Naver plans to integrate this cost-effective, high-performance AI across its key services, accelerating its On-Service AI strategy?embedding AI into all of its platforms while optimizing operating costs. The new HyperCLOVA X model will be integrated into Naver’s conversational AI chatbot, CLOVA X, in March. It will also be made available to enterprise customers through Naver Cloud Platform. Naver is also working to enhance AI planning and reasoning capabilities, with plans to launch a conversational AI version of HyperCLOVA X in the second half of 2025.

data.title: AsiaPhos Forecasts Lower Profit in 2024
data.description:
AsiaPhos expects to report a lower profit for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024, compared with the previous year, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The company attributed this to the provision of impairment made on receivables from a previous subsidiary.

The company will release its financial results on or before March 1.

data.title: SeAH Besteel wins certification for carbon footprint system
data.description: SeAH Besteel Corp. was granted an international standard for verifying the carbon footprint of special steel products, known as ISO 14067. ISO 14067, established by the International Organization for Standardization (ISO), provides a framework for measuring and quantifying carbon emissions across a product’s life cycle. “The certification proves that our carbon emission tracking and management systems at its local facilities are reliable,” the company said.

data.title: Market Chatter: Hanwha Ocean Delivers 200th LNG Carrier
data.description:
South Korean shipbuilder Hanwha Ocean delivered its 200th liquefied natural gas (LNG) carrier, Yonhap News reported Thursday.

The vessel was handed over to SK Shipping and will be used in Qatar Energy's North Field Expansion project. The carrier, featuring environment-friendly technology and Hanwha's smart ship solution, minimizes emissions and enhances operational efficiency, the report said.

Hanwha Ocean, which delivered its first LNG carrier in 1995, reached the 200-unit mark in just nine years after hitting 100 deliveries in 2016, it said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

Chinese Shares Higher, Led by Hardware Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: Chinese shares are higher at the midday break, with the Shanghai Composite Index adding 0.8% at 3376.72. The Shenzhen Composite Index gains 1.4% at 2084.59 and ChiNext Price Index is 2.2% higher at 2275.32. Investor sentiment has been boosted by positive tech earnings, with Alibaba posting its highest revenue growth in more than a year. Among major stocks, hardware companies are leading the gains as investors expect higher chip demand as AI adoption widens. SMIC adds 5.7% and Hygon Information Technology gains 16%. Banks are lower; China Construction Bank is 1.4% lower and Industrial & Commercial Bank of China, declines 1.3%. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Rex International Flags Net Loss for 2024; Shares Plunge 18%
data.description:
Rex International is expecting to book a net loss for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The company has attributed this to impairment of goodwill, oil and gas properties and exploration and evaluation assets.

Shares of the oil and gas exploration company fell over 18% in recent trading.

New Zealand Shares Fall 1%, Complete Worst Week Since December — Market Talk
data.description: New Zealand's NZX-50 finished 1.0% lower at 12752.58, rounding out its worst week of 2025, marking a fourth consecutive decline. The benchmark index fell at the open amid a soft lead from U.S. equities, spending the entirety of Friday's session in the red, to complete a 1.8% weekly drop. Telecommunications provider Spark led Friday's broad-based declines, tumbling 19% after cutting its annual earning guidance following falls in 1H revenue, earnings and profit. Financial and property stocks were also notable losers. The NZX-50 ended the week at its lowest closing level since Dec. 12, and 2.7% lower so far this year. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Brazilian Critical Minerals Updates Brazil Rare Earth Elements Project's Mineral Resource Estimate, Shares Rise 13%
data.description:
Brazilian Critical Minerals updated its mineral resource estimate for the Ema rare earth elements project in Brazil, with the starter zone at the project now containing 341 million tonnes at 746 parts per million total rare earth oxides in the indicated and inferred category, according to a Friday Australian bourse filing.

The starter zone contains 73% or 248 million tonnes at 759 ppm grade of total rare earth oxides in the indicated category, an increase of 97% over the previous estimate.

Results from the 2024 drill program were able to convert the estimate from inferred to indicated category, the filing added.

The firm is finalizing a scoping study, which it plans to release during the first quarter.

Its updated global mineral resource estimate is 943 million tonnes at 716 ppm grade of total rare earth oxides.

The firm's shares rose 13% in recent trading on Friday.

data.title: IMF says further Thai rate reduction would support inflation
data.description:
A further reduction in Thailand's policy interest rate would support inflation and improve the debt-servicing capacity of borrowers, the International Monetary Fund said.

Given high uncertainty around the economic outlook, authorities should stand ready to adjust monetary policy as needed, the Washington-based IMF said in a statementdated February 20.

It said there was little risk of a rate cut leading to increased borrowing given lending conditions remain tight in the economy.

Central bank independence with clear communication of policy moves was key to maintaining the credibility and effectiveness of monetary policy in anchoring inflation expectations, the IMF said.

In December, the Bank of Thailand left its main interest rate (THCBIR=ECI) at 2.25% following a surprise quarter-point cut in October.

Last month, the central bank governor told Reuters the current policy rate remained suitable, given high household debt, even though growth could be below 2.9% this year.

The next policy review is scheduled for February 26.

The IMF forecast Thailand's economic growth at 2.9% this year, unchanged from a report in November.

Southeast Asia's second-largest economy expanded 2.5% last year, lagging regional peers.
